title: Hemipilia takasago-montana
taxonomy: Hemipilia takasago-montana is a species of orchid, more specifically belonging to the genus Hemipilia. The Hemipilia genus is part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This genus is further classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes various terrestrial orchids. The subfamily Orchidoideae is a part of the Asparagales order, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Asparagales order is one of the major groups within the monocot clade of flowering plants.

intro: Hemipilia takasago-montana, also known as Takasago Hemipilia, is a rare species of orchid native to the mountainous regions of Japan. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hemipilia takasago-montana include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Japanese medicine, Hemipilia takasago-montana is often prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
